key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $4.04, up 33.3% YoY from $3.03
Operating Income of $1.38 billion, up 31.0% YoY
Cash provided by operating activities (6 months) of $3.67 billion, up 46.4% YoY
qoq_changes: Revenue increased sequentially to $23.47 billion from an implied $22.24 billion in the prior quarter.
Net income increased sequentially to $956 million from an implied $824 million in the prior quarter.
Separation and other costs increased to $213 million in the current quarter.
new_risks: Incurred $213 million in separation and other costs, indicating potential execution risks related to ongoing corporate restructuring.
Purchased transportation costs rose 7% YoY to $5.89 billion, highlighting exposure to third-party rate inflation.
management_tone: Bullish, as the company successfully drove 29% net income growth and expanded operating margins despite incurring significant separation costs.
analyst_note: FDX demonstrated strong operating leverage this quarter, translating a 6.8% top-line increase into a 29% jump in net income. The presence of $213 million in separation costs suggests management is aggressively pursuing its structural cost-reduction initiatives, which should further support margin expansion in the second half of the fiscal year.
